title: dactylorhiza incarnata var. dunensis
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata var. dunensis, commonly known as the Dun Orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid that belongs to the Orchidaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The Dun Orchid is believed to have various health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure, improving respiratory health, and exhibiting potential antiviral properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
